title: ActiveCampaign vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign and GetResponse. We break down pricing, features, and performance to help you choose the right email marketing tool.
h1: ActiveCampaign vs GetResponse: Detailed Feature & Price Comparison
intro: ActiveCampaign and GetResponse are two established email marketing platforms with different strengths. ActiveCampaign excels at advanced automation and CRM functionality, while GetResponse offers a broader feature set including webinars and online courses. Your choice depends on whether you prioritize deep sales automation or multi-channel marketing capabilities.

comparison_table.tool_a: Lead scoring, site tracking, SMS, dynamic content
comparison_table.tool_b: Webinars, landing pages, online courses, conversion funnels

choose_a_if: You need sophisticated lead scoring and sales automation workflows
CRM functionality integrated with email marketing is essential
You want SMS marketing included in your platform
Your business is B2B-focused with complex sales processes
choose_b_if: You want to start with a free plan before committing financially
You host webinars or sell online courses
You need landing page and funnel-building tools built-in
You prefer an all-in-one platform over specialized tools
You're a beginner looking for an accessible starting point

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ActiveCampaign is superior for sales automation and CRM integration, making it better for B2B companies and complex sales processes. GetResponse excels as an all-in-one platform with webinars and course hosting, making it ideal for digital product creators. Compare your specific requirements before deciding.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or GetResponse?
faq.a: GetResponse is more affordable for getting started since it offers a free plan for up to 500 subscribers. ActiveCampaign has no free tier, starting at $15/month. However, pricing depends on your subscriber count and features needed—both can scale significantly as you grow.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms allow you to export your contact lists and email templates. Both ActiveCampaign and GetResponse support standard data export formats. Plan the migration carefully to ensure automation workflows are properly recreated in your new platform.
